---
title: My fantastic presentation
subtitle: My subtitle
author: My name
date: 2026-04-22
date-format: full
format: 
  revealjs:
    include-in-header:
      - text: <link rel = "shortcut icon" href = "ucsf_favicon.svg" />
    theme: ucsf.scss
    embed-resources: false
    slide_level: 2
    slide-number: true
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: '#052049'
      data-background-image: 'ucsf_logo.png'
      data-background-size: '10%'       
      data-background-position: 'top 3% left 1%' 
---

# Introduction

## Examples of basic Markdown

- this is **bold**
- this is *italic*
- this is `typewriter font`
- this is a [link](https://ucsf.edu)

## Example of a footnote

- this is a reveal.js theme for UCSF

::: aside
UCSF: University of California, San Francisco.
:::

## Example of an image

![To care for the future, we must safeguard research.](example_image.jpg){height=400 fig-align='center'}

## Example of a block quote

- the I in PRIDE is:

> Integrity: To be honest, trustworthy and ethical, always doing the right thing, without compromising the truth, and being fair and sincere.

# Methods

## Some points

- point 1
- point 2
- point 3

# What is Quarto?

## Example of a warning

::: callout-warning
Dragging things around in PowerPoint is tedious, and can distract you from the goal of improving your content.
:::

## Example of a tip

::: callout-tip
Consider using Quarto to make slides. 😊 Writing in Markdown may help you focus on improving your content.
:::

## Resources

- the [Quarto guide](https://quarto.org/docs/guide/)
- Quarto's [guide to making slides with reveal.js](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/)
- UCSF Library's [Data Science and Open Scholarship Team](https://www.library.ucsf.edu/ask-an-expert/dsos-team/)
